scores.justification: Methodology is rigorous and claims are calibrated to evidence (pre-registered tests, power floors, post-hoc flags, an honestly withdrawn robustness claim); the one named weakness - the class-level trainability comparison remains ordering-only at the shipped budget - does not undermine the headline phase-order claim.

Well-organized with a clean 27-page build (0 overfull hboxes, 0 broken refs) and a now-extractable abstract, but the headline Figure 1 caption carries a transposed rows/columns description contradicting the figure's own axis labels - a caption defect a reviewer will notice on first read.

Solid but deliberately narrow for the Cooperative AI community: an NE-transparent parametric benchmark, a per-cell baseline methodological lesson, two honestly retired predictors, and a machine-verdicted social-trap target constitute useful methodological infrastructure in a niche the paper itself bounds.

The parametric phase-diagram-with-computable-NE framing is new and the closest prior line (OpenSpiel; PSRO/NashConv exploitability evaluation) is now engaged accurately, with the measurable-equilibrium-convergence credit explicitly ceded and the claim scoped to the parametric-family property.

Code, seeds, hyperparameter pins, frozen scenario IDs, parity manifest, and per-figure recipes are supplied, and this review re-derived the headline statistics and Figure 2 from the committed artifacts at HEAD; held below full because the baseline-checkpoint hosting is still in progress and one statistic traces to a non-regenerable PR recomputation.

findings.rationale: The NeurIPS checklist expects the hosted-baselines pathway resolved, not 'in progress'.
findings.suggested_fix: Complete or remove the HuggingFace hosting promise before submission.

findings.rationale: Figure 1 caption's panel-layout clause is transposed relative to the rendered axes.
findings.suggested_fix: Swap to 'rows are beta and columns are kappa'.
